title: Pungent Greek Buckwheat Flour Tzatziki
description: A light and refreshing greek dish featuring buckwheat flour, perfect for a warm spring evening. This jain-friendly recipe combines the tangy flavors of yogurt and cucumber with the nutty taste of buckwheat flour, all while being easy to make and requiring minimal cooking time.
instructions: Combine the cucumber, garlic, and red onion in a colander and season with salt. Let it sit for at least 10 minutes to allow the water to be drawn out of the vegetables.
After 10 minutes, squeeze out as much water as possible from the vegetables using a paper towel.
In a blender or food processor, combine the drained vegetables, Greek yogurt, buckwheat flour, fresh dill, lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper, and blend until smooth.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed. If using a flame-free flamb52 substitute, carefully add it to the mixture and stir to combine.
Cover the mixture and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together and the mixture to chill.
When ready to serve, give the mixture a good stir and scoop it into individual serving cups or a large serving bowl.
